dataset.full_name: A dataset of synthetic, maturation-informed magnetic resonance images of the human fetal brain
dataset.description: Multi-tissue segmentation of the fetal brain from synthetic T2-weighted MRI.
dataset.target_structure: external CSF, grey matter, white matter, ventricles, cerebellum, deep grey matter, brainstem, corpus callosum
dataset.acquisition_info: Synthetic images simulating 1.5T/3T GE scanners using T2-weighted Single-Shot Fast Spin Echo (SS-FSE) sequences. Parameters include: slice thickness 3.0-5.0 mm, in-plane resolution 0.5 mm isotropic, TR/TE 2000-3500 ms/120 ms, flip angle 90°.
dataset.annotation_info: High-resolution multi-tissue annotations from the FeTA dataset with refined annotations, which were manually corrected by clinical experts. The labels were automatically propagated to the simulated low-resolution series via nearest-neighbor interpolation.
dataset.data_source: Synthetic dataset generated from the public Fetal Tissue Annotations (FeTA) dataset using the FaBiAN v2.0 simulation framework.
dataset.detailed_description: This dataset consists of 70 synthetic T2-weighted MRI scans of the developing fetal brain, generated using the FaBiAN v2.0 numerical phantom. The images simulate realistic clinical low-resolution acquisitions from 1.5T and 3T GE scanners. The corresponding segmentation masks delineate nine tissue classes: background, external CSF, grey matter, white matter, ventricles, cerebellum, deep grey matter, brainstem, and corpus callosum. The synthetic data is derived from high-resolution annotations of real clinical subjects from the FeTA dataset, with gestational ages ranging from 20.0 to 34.8 weeks.
dataset.clinical_relevance: Accurate multi-tissue segmentation of the fetal brain is crucial for quantifying brain development, detecting abnormalities, and planning postnatal interventions. It enables the study of normal and pathological brain maturation in utero.
